babyLearning.en: growing round and ready, settling lower
babyLearning.hi: gol aur taiyaar hote hue, neeche bas rahe hain

title.en: Fear And Excitement Feel The Same
title.hi: Dar Aur Utsaah Ek Jaise Lagte Hain

insight.en: In the body, fear and excitement are almost identical — the racing heart, the fluttering belly. What changes everything is the story you tell about it.
insight.hi: Sharir mein, dar aur utsaah lagbhag ek jaise hote hain — tej dhadkan, pet mein titliyaan. Sab kuch tabhi badalta hai jab aap iske baare mein khud ko kahani kya sunaati hain.

expanded.en: As these final days approach, you may feel a flutter you cannot quite name — is it dread, or is it longing? The truth is your body cannot tell them apart; both quicken the pulse and lift the breath. So when the nerves rise, you do not have to push them away. You can gently rename them: not 'I am afraid,' but 'I am ready, and something wonderful is near.' The very same energy that feels like fear is also the energy of meeting your child. Let it be excitement instead. You are allowed to feel both at once.
expanded.hi: Jaise yeh aakhri din paas aate hain, aap ek aisi halchal mehsoos kar sakti hain jise aap theek se naam nahi de paatin — yeh ghabraahat hai, ya tadap? Sach yeh hai ki aapka sharir dono mein farq nahi kar paata; dono dhadkan tej karte hain aur saans uthaate hain. To jab nerves uthein, aapko unhe door dhakelne ki zaroorat nahi. Aap unhe pyaar se naya naam de sakti hain: 'main darti hoon' nahi, balki 'main taiyaar hoon, aur kuch sundar paas hai.' Wahi energy jo dar jaisi lagti hai, woh apne bachche se milne ki energy bhi hai. Use utsaah banne dein. Aapko ek saath dono mehsoos karne ki ijaazat hai.

deepDive.en: Psychologists call it 'anxiety reappraisal' — simply telling yourself 'I am excited' rather than 'calm down' measurably improves performance, because the body is already primed and only the label needs to shift.
deepDive.hi: Psychologists ise 'anxiety reappraisal' kehte hain — khud ko 'shaant ho jao' kehne ke bajaaye bas 'main excited hoon' kehna performance ko maapne layak behtar karta hai, kyunki sharir to pehle se taiyaar hai aur sirf label badalne ki zaroorat hoti hai.

remember.en: The same flutter that feels like fear can be named excitement.
remember.hi: Wahi halchal jo dar jaisi lagti hai, use utsaah kaha ja sakta hai.

title.en: The Lion And His Own Shadow
title.hi: Sher Aur Uski Apni Parchhaai

summary.en: A young lion learns the fierce thing chasing him was only his own shadow.
summary.hi: Ek jawaan sher seekhta hai ki uska peecha karti khaufnaak cheez bas uski apni parchhaai thi.

body.en:
A young lion was walking home across the open plain one evening when he glanced behind him and froze. A huge, dark shape was stretched across the ground, following his every step. He broke into a run, and the dark shape ran too. He leapt, and it leapt. He roared, and it loomed larger still. Terrified, he raced faster and faster, certain some great beast was about to seize him.

At last, exhausted, he stopped beneath a tall tree to catch his breath. And there, in the shade, the dark shape simply vanished. Puzzled, he stepped back into the open — and it returned at once, long across the grass. Slowly he understood. The fearsome thing chasing him was nothing but his own shadow, stretched long by the setting sun.

He laughed at himself then, and walked home calmly. The shadow followed, as shadows do — but it never frightened him again.
body.hi:
Ek jawaan sher ek shaam khule maidaan se ghar laut raha tha jab usne peeche mudkar dekha aur sehm gaya. Ek vishaal, kaali aakruti zameen par faili thi, uske har kadam ka peecha karti. Woh daudne laga, aur kaali aakruti bhi daudi. Woh kooda, aur woh bhi koodi. Woh dahaada, aur woh aur badi ho gayi. Dare hue, woh tej aur tej bhaaga, use yaqeen tha ki koi bada darinda use pakadne hi waala hai.

Aakhir mein, thakkar, woh saans lene ke liye ek oonche ped ke neeche ruka. Aur wahan, chhaaya mein, kaali aakruti bas gaayab ho gayi. Hairaan hokar, woh wapas khule mein kadam rakha — aur woh turant laut aayi, ghaas par lambi. Dheere-dheere use samajh aaya. Uska peecha karti khaufnaak cheez uski apni parchhaai ke siva kuch nahi thi, dhalte sooraj se lambi khinchi hui.

Woh tab khud par hansa, aur shaanti se ghar chala. Parchhaai peeche aayi, jaise parchhaaiyaan aati hain — par usne use phir kabhi nahi daraaya.

title.en: Tell your baby about the courage the two of you will find together when you finally meet.
title.hi: Apne baby ko us himmat ke baare mein bataayein jo aap dono milkar paayenge jab aap aakhir mein milenge.

motivation.en: Naming your courage aloud helps you trust it is already there.
motivation.hi: Apni himmat ko bolkar kehna aapko bharosa dilaata hai ki woh pehle se hi maujood hai.

title.en: Raga Ramkali
title.hi: Raag Ramkali

description.en: Dawn raga for quiet courage at daybreak
description.hi: Bhor ka raag, subah ki shaant himmat ke liye

affirmation.en: As this dawn melody rises, so does a quiet courage in me, ready to welcome you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh bhor ki dhun uthti hai, waise hi mujhmein ek shaant himmat uthti hai, tumhara swagat karne ko taiyaar.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

title.en: Breathing The Nerves Down
title.hi: Nerves Ko Saans Se Shaant Karna

content.en: When the flutter rises, breathe in for four counts, then breathe out slowly for six. The long out-breath tells your body there is no danger here — only anticipation.
content.hi: Jab halchal uthe, chaar tak ginte hue saans andar lein, phir chhah tak dheere saans baahar chhodein. Lambi baahar wali saans aapke sharir ko batati hai ki yahaan koi khatra nahi — sirf intezaar hai.

remember.en: A slow out-breath turns alarm into anticipation.
remember.hi: Ek dheemi baahar wali saans ghabraahat ko intezaar mein badal deti hai.

babyLearning.en: soft and strong, practising for the world
babyLearning.hi: naram aur mazboot, duniya ke liye abhyaas karte hue

title.en: Your Body Was Made For This
title.hi: Aapka Sharir Iske Liye Bana Hai

insight.en: Long before any book or class, your body already holds an ancient, quiet wisdom about bringing a child into the world.
insight.hi: Kisi bhi kitaab ya class se bahut pehle, aapka sharir pehle se ek puraani, shaant samajh rakhta hai ki ek bachche ko duniya mein kaise laana hai.

expanded.en: It is easy, in these last weeks, to feel you must learn and control everything. But your body carries a wisdom older than any instruction — the same wisdom that has carried mothers across every generation before you. You do not have to summon strength from nothing; it is already woven into you, waiting. Trusting your body is not the same as having no fears. It simply means letting some of the weight rest on something deeper than your worried thoughts. When the moment comes, you will not be doing this alone — your body has been quietly preparing all along.
expanded.hi: In aakhri hafton mein, yeh mehsoos karna aasaan hai ki aapko sab kuch seekhna aur control karna hai. Par aapka sharir kisi bhi nirdesh se puraani samajh rakhta hai — wahi samajh jo aapse pehle har peedhi ki maaon ko thaame rahi hai. Aapko kahin se taakat khoj kar laane ki zaroorat nahi; woh pehle se aap mein buni hui hai, intezaar karti. Apne sharir par bharosa karna iska matlab yeh nahi ki koi dar na ho. Iska bas matlab hai apni chinta bhari soch se kuch gehri cheez par thoda bojh tikne dena. Jab woh pal aayega, aap yeh akele nahi kar rahi hongi — aapka sharir chupchaap shuru se taiyaari karta raha hai.

deepDive.en: Experts in maternal health note that confidence and a sense of safety can ease the whole experience of birth, while fear tends to tighten the body. Trust, in this sense, is not naive — it is gently practical.
deepDive.hi: Maa ki sehat ke jaankaar kehte hain ki aatmavishwaas aur surakshit mehsoos karna janm ke poore anubhav ko aasaan kar sakta hai, jabki dar sharir ko kasta hai. Bharosa, is maayne mein, naadaani nahi — yeh pyaar se vyavahaarik hai.

remember.en: A wisdom older than words is already woven into you.
remember.hi: Shabdon se puraani ek samajh pehle se aap mein buni hui hai.

title.en: The Brave Little Mouse
title.hi: Bahaadur Nanha Chooha

summary.en: A tiny mouse outwits a fearsome cat with courage far bigger than his size.
summary.hi: Ek nanha chooha apni jisamat se kahin badi himmat se ek khaufnaak billi ko maat de deta hai.

body.en:
In an old granary lived a family of mice, and a sleek, sharp-eyed cat who prowled there each night. The mice grew so afraid they hardly dared leave their hole, and their little ones went hungry. The biggest, strongest mice all agreed something must be done — yet none of them moved.

Then the smallest mouse of all stepped forward. 'If we tie a little bell around the cat's neck,' he said, 'we will hear her coming and slip away in time.' The others laughed — who could ever creep close enough to a cat to bell it? But the little mouse only nodded and waited.

That night, while the cat dozed full and lazy after her supper, the tiny mouse crept up on trembling paws and looped a thread with a bell gently round her neck. From then on, the soft ring warned them always, and the mice lived in peace. The smallest of them had carried the biggest courage of all.
body.hi:
Ek purane anaaj-ghar mein choohon ka ek parivar rehta tha, aur ek chikni, tez-nazar billi jo har raat wahan ghoomti. Choohe itne dar gaye ki apni bil se nikalne ki himmat hi na karte, aur unke bachche bhookhe reh jaate. Sabse bade, sabse mazboot choohe sehmat the ki kuch karna chahiye — phir bhi koi hila nahi.

Tabhi sabse chhota chooha aage aaya. 'Agar hum billi ke gale mein ek chhoti ghanti baandh dein,' usne kaha, 'to hum use aate sun lenge aur samay rehte nikal jaayenge.' baaki hanse — bhala kaun billi ke itne paas ghisak sakta hai ki use ghanti baandhe? Par nanha chooha bas sir hilaakar intezaar karta raha.

Us raat, jab billi apne bhojan ke baad bhari aur sust soyi padi thi, nanha chooha kaanpte panjon par chadha aur ek dhaage mein bandhi ghanti pyaar se uske gale mein daal di. Tab se, woh halki ghanti hamesha unhe chetaati rahi, aur choohe shaanti se jeene lage. Un sab mein sabse chhote ne sabse badi himmat dikhaayi thi.

title.en: Tell your baby how the two of you will face the hard days as a team, hand in hand.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap dono milkar, ek team ki tarah, mushkil dinon ka saamna kaise karenge.

motivation.en: Promising to face things together makes both of you braver before you even start.
motivation.hi: Saath saamna karne ka vaada aap dono ko shuru karne se pehle hi zyada himmati bana deta hai.

title.en: Strength Rising From Within
title.hi: Andar Se Uthti Taakat

description.en: A short meditation to feel your quiet strength
description.hi: Apni shaant taakat ko mehsoos karne ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: With every breath, I feel a steady strength rising up from deep within me.
affirmation.hi: Har saans ke saath, main apne andar gehre se uthti ek sthir taakat mehsoos karti hoon.

introduction.en: Sit comfortably and let your eyes close. Take three slow breaths and feel your feet, your seat, the steady ground beneath you. With each breath in, imagine a calm, warm strength rising from the base of your body up to your heart, the way roots feed a tall, unshaken tree.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur aankhein band hone dein. Teen dheemi saansein lein aur apne pair, apni seat, apne neeche ki sthir zameen ko mehsoos karein. Har saans andar lete waqt, sochein ki ek shaant, garm taakat aapke sharir ke neeche se uthkar aapke dil tak aa rahi hai, jaise jadein ek oonche, na-hilne wale ped ko poshan deti hain.

closingThought.en: That strength was always yours. Carry the feeling of it gently with you into the day.
closingThought.hi: Woh taakat hamesha aapki thi. Uske ehsaas ko pyaar se apne saath din mein le jaayein.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.en: My Body Knows The Way
title.hi: Mera Sharir Raasta Jaanta Hai

content.en: I do not have to figure out everything with my mind. My body holds a wisdom I can lean on and trust.
content.hi: Mujhe apne dimaag se sab kuch samajhna nahi padta. Mera sharir ek samajh rakhta hai jis par main tik sakti aur bharosa kar sakti hoon.

remember.en: You can trust the wisdom your body already holds.
remember.hi: Aap us samajh par bharosa kar sakti hain jo aapka sharir pehle se rakhta hai.

babyLearning.en: snug and curled, listening to your voice
babyLearning.hi: aaram se simta hua, aapki aawaz sunta hua

title.en: Brave Is Not The Absence Of Fear
title.hi: Bahaaduri Dar Ki Anupasthiti Nahi Hai

insight.en: Courage has never meant feeling no fear. It means feeling the fear fully — and choosing to step forward anyway.
insight.hi: Himmat ka matlab kabhi yeh nahi raha ki dar mehsoos hi na ho. Iska matlab hai dar ko poori tarah mehsoos karna — aur phir bhi aage badhna chunna.

expanded.en: Somewhere we picked up the idea that brave people are simply unafraid — that if you feel fear, you are not strong enough. But the truth is gentler and far kinder: every brave act in the world was done by someone whose heart was pounding. Courage is not the absence of fear; it is fear walked through with love on the other side. You do not need to wait until you feel fearless to feel brave. The very fact that you are scared and still moving forward, still loving, still here — that is courage in its purest form.
expanded.hi: Kahin se humne yeh vichaar pakad liya ki bahaadur log bas nidar hote hain — ki agar aap dar mehsoos karti hain to aap kaafi mazboot nahi. Par sach narm aur kahin zyada dayaalu hai: duniya mein har bahaadur kaam kisi aise ne kiya jiska dil tej dhadak raha tha. Himmat dar ki anupasthiti nahi hai; yeh dar ke beech se pyaar ke saath guzar jaana hai jiske doosri taraf koi apna ho. Aapko bahaadur mehsoos karne ke liye nidar hone ka intezaar karne ki zaroorat nahi. Yahi tathya ki aap dari hui hain aur phir bhi aage badh rahi hain, phir bhi pyaar kar rahi hain, phir bhi yahaan hain — yahi himmat ka sabse shuddh roop hai.

deepDive.en: Researchers who study courage find it is not the opposite of fear but a companion to it — the willingness to act in service of something we love, even while afraid. Fear, in fact, is often the sign that something deeply matters.
deepDive.hi: Himmat ka adhyayan karne wale researchers paate hain ki yeh dar ka ulta nahi balki uska saathi hai — kisi aisi cheez ki sewa mein kaam karne ki ichha jise hum pyaar karte hain, dare hue bhi. Dar, asal mein, aksar is baat ki nishaani hai ki kuch gehraai se maayne rakhta hai.

remember.en: Brave is feeling the fear and stepping forward anyway.
remember.hi: Bahaaduri dar ko mehsoos karna aur phir bhi aage badhna hai.

title.en: Hanuman's Leap
title.hi: Hanuman Ki Chhalaang

summary.en: Hanuman remembers his own forgotten strength and leaps across the great ocean.
summary.hi: Hanuman apni bhooli hui taakat ko yaad karte hain aur vishaal samundar paar chhalaang lagaate hain.

body.en:
When the great task came — to leap clear across the wide ocean to the distant shore — even the mighty vanaras fell silent. The sea stretched on without end, and one by one they doubted they could cross it. Hanuman too sat quietly, unsure of his own power, his strength half-forgotten.

Then the wise old Jambavan came and sat beside him. 'You do not yet know what you carry,' he said gently. 'You are the son of the wind. The strength you are searching for is not somewhere far away — it has been inside you all along.' Slowly, as the words sank in, Hanuman felt something stir and rise within his chest, a power he had forgotten he possessed.

He stood, grew vast as a mountain, and with his heart set on his purpose, he leapt — soaring over the endless sea toward the far shore. He had not gained new strength that day. He had only remembered the strength that was always his.
body.hi:
Jab woh mahaan kaam aaya — chaude samundar ke paar door ke kinare tak chhalaang lagaana — to mazboot vanar bhi chup ho gaye. Samundar bina ant ke faila tha, aur ek-ek karke unhe shak hua ki woh use paar kar paayenge. Hanuman bhi shaanti se baithe the, apni khud ki shakti ke baare mein anishchit, unki taakat aadhi bhooli hui.

Tabhi buddhimaan boodhe Jambavan aaye aur unke paas baith gaye. 'Tum abhi nahi jaante ki tum kya rakhte ho,' unhone pyaar se kaha. 'Tum pawan ke putra ho. Jis taakat ko tum khoj rahe ho woh kahin door nahi hai — woh hamesha se tumhare andar hi rahi hai.' Dheere-dheere, jaise yeh shabd andar utre, Hanuman ne apne seene mein kuch hilta aur uthta mehsoos kiya, ek shakti jise woh bhool gaye the ki unke paas hai.

Woh khade hue, ek pahaad jitne vishaal ho gaye, aur apne uddeshya par dil tikaakar, unhone chhalaang lagaayi — ant-heen samundar ke ooper door ke kinare ki taraf udte hue. Us din unhone nayi taakat nahi paayi thi. Unhone bas us taakat ko yaad kiya jo hamesha unki thi.

title.en: Share your quiet hopes for your baby as their arrival draws so very near.
title.hi: Apni shaant ummeedein apne baby ke saath baantein jab unka aana itna paas aa gaya hai.

motivation.en: Hopes spoken now become the first soft welcome your baby feels.
motivation.hi: Abhi kahi gayi ummeedein woh pehla naram swagat ban jaati hain jo aapka baby mehsoos karta hai.

title.en: I Am Brave Enough For This
title.hi: Main Iske Liye Kaafi Bahaadur Hoon

description.hi: Himmat se bhara ek bond affirmation

affirmation.en: I do not need to be unafraid to be brave. With you in my arms ahead, I am brave enough for whatever comes.
affirmation.hi: Bahaadur hone ke liye mujhe nidar hone ki zaroorat nahi. Aage tumhe apni baahon mein lekar, main jo bhi aaye uske liye kaafi bahaadur hoon.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.en: When Heartburn Steals Your Appetite
title.hi: Jab Seene Ki Jalan Bhookh Chura Le

content.en: If a full plate brings heartburn this late, try small, frequent meals instead — a bowl of curd, a few soft idlis, a banana. Eating little and often is gentler on a crowded belly than three big meals.
content.hi: Agar is aakhri daur mein bhari thaali seene mein jalan laaye, to badi thaali ke bajaaye chhota-chhota baar-baar khaayein — ek katori dahi, kuch naram idli, ek kela. Thoda aur baar-baar khaana ek bhare pet par teen badi thaaliyon se zyada naram padta hai.

remember.en: Small, frequent meals sit easier on a crowded belly.
remember.hi: Chhota, baar-baar khaana ek bhare pet par zyada aaraam se baithta hai.

babyLearning.en: resting low, getting ready to meet you
babyLearning.hi: neeche aaram karta, aapse milne ko taiyaar hota

title.en: Breathe Into The Unknown
title.hi: Anjaan Mein Saans Lein

insight.en: You cannot know exactly how the days ahead will unfold — and you do not need to. You only need to meet each breath as it comes.
insight.hi: Aap theek se nahi jaan sakti ki aage ke din kaise khulenge — aur aapko zaroorat bhi nahi. Aapko bas har saans ka saamna karna hai jaise woh aaye.

expanded.en: So much of the worry in these final days comes from trying to picture exactly what will happen and when. But the unknown is not your enemy; it is simply the part of the story not yet written. You do not have to solve it all in advance. When the mind races ahead into a hundred what-ifs, you can always come home to this one breath, this one moment, which is the only place life is ever actually lived. Breathe into the not-knowing. You are allowed to walk forward without seeing the whole path — most of life's most beautiful turns were never on the map.
expanded.hi: In aakhri dinon ki itni saari chinta theek se yeh tasveer banane ki koshish se aati hai ki kya hoga aur kab. Par anjaan aapka dushman nahi hai; yeh bas kahani ka woh hissa hai jo abhi likha nahi gaya. Aapko sab kuch pehle se sulhana nahi hai. Jab mann sau what-ifs mein aage daudta hai, aap hamesha is ek saans, is ek pal mein laut sakti hain, jo ekmaatra jagah hai jahaan zindagi sach mein jee jaati hai. Na-jaanne mein saans lein. Aapko poora raasta dekhe bina aage badhne ki ijaazat hai — zindagi ke sabse sundar mod kabhi naqshe par nahi the.

deepDive.en: Mindfulness teachers note that most distress lives in imagined futures, not the present moment. Returning attention to a single breath is one of the simplest ways to step out of spiralling 'what-if' thinking.
deepDive.hi: Mindfulness sikhaane wale kehte hain ki zyadatar pareshani kalpit bhavishya mein basti hai, vartmaan pal mein nahi. Dhyan ko ek hi saans par laut laana ghoomte 'what-if' soch se baahar nikalne ke sabse saadi tareekon mein se ek hai.

remember.en: You can walk forward without seeing the whole path.
remember.hi: Aap poora raasta dekhe bina aage badh sakti hain.

title.en: When The Hare Heard A Thud
title.hi: Jab Khargosh Ne Ek Dhamaaka Suna

summary.en: A frightened hare sets the whole forest fleeing, until a calm lion checks the truth.
summary.hi: Ek dara hua khargosh poore jungle ko bhaga deta hai, jab tak ek shaant sher sach ki jaanch nahi karta.

body.en:
A hare was dozing beneath a ripe palm tree when a heavy fruit fell with a great thud right behind him. Startled awake, and certain the earth itself was breaking apart, he leapt up and bolted, crying, 'The ground is splitting! Run, run!' A deer saw him fleeing and ran too. Then a boar, then a buffalo, then a whole herd of elephants — until half the forest was thundering along in blind panic, none of them knowing why.

At last a wise old lion stood in their path and roared them to a halt. 'Who among you actually saw the ground split?' he asked. One by one they pointed back, until it came to the trembling hare. 'I heard it,' he admitted, 'just behind me, near the palm tree.'

The lion walked him calmly back to the spot — and there lay a single fallen fruit. The hare's whole terror had grown from one small sound he never stopped to understand. Sometimes the fearful thing is not nearly as big as the panic around it.
body.hi:
Ek khargosh ek pake taad ke ped ke neeche oongh raha tha jab ek bhaari phal uske theek peeche ek bade dhamaake ke saath gira. Haadbada kar jaaga, aur yaqeen kar ki dharti hi toot rahi hai, woh uchhal kar bhaag nikla, chillaata, 'Zameen phat rahi hai! Bhaago, bhaago!' Ek hiran ne use bhaagte dekha aur woh bhi bhaaga. Phir ek soor, phir ek bhains, phir haathiyon ka poora jhund — jab tak aadha jungle andhe darr mein daudta na laga, koi nahi jaanta kyun.

Aakhir mein ek buddhimaan boodha sher unki raah mein khada hua aur dahaad kar unhe rok diya. 'Tum mein se kisne sach mein zameen ko phatte dekha?' usne poocha. Ek-ek karke sab peeche ishaara karte gaye, jab tak baat kaanpte khargosh tak na aayi. 'Maine use suna,' usne maana, 'theek mere peeche, taad ke ped ke paas.'

Sher use shaanti se wapas us jagah le gaya — aur wahaan ek akela gira hua phal pada tha. Khargosh ka poora aatank ek chhoti aawaz se uga tha jise woh kabhi rukkar samajhne nahi gaya. Kabhi-kabhi darawni cheez uske around ke darr jitni badi nahi hoti.

title.en: Gently tell your baby that you are letting go of your fears so you can welcome them with an open heart.
title.hi: Apne baby ko pyaar se bataayein ki aap apne dar chhod rahi hain taaki aap unhe khule dil se aapna sakein.

motivation.en: Naming a fear so you can release it makes room for more love.
motivation.hi: Ek dar ko naam dena taaki aap use chhod sakein, zyada pyaar ke liye jagah banaata hai.

title.en: Letting The Worry Float Away
title.hi: Chinta Ko Behne Dena

description.en: A short meditation to release what you cannot hold
description.hi: Jo aap thaam nahi sakti use chhodne ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: I gently set down what I cannot control, and rest in this one safe breath.
affirmation.hi: Jo main control nahi kar sakti use main pyaar se rakh deti hoon, aur is ek surakshit saans mein aaram karti hoon.

introduction.en: Settle into a comfortable seat and close your eyes. Take three slow breaths. Picture yourself sitting beside a quiet river. As each worry comes to mind, imagine placing it gently on a leaf and watching the water carry it softly downstream, until your mind grows calm and open.
introduction.hi: Ek aaramdayak jagah par baithein aur aankhein band karein. Teen dheemi saansein lein. Khud ko ek shaant nadi ke kinare baitha hua sochein. Jaise har chinta mann mein aaye, use pyaar se ek patte par rakhte aur paani ko use dheere se neeche behaate hue dekhte sochein, jab tak aapka mann shaant aur khula na ho jaaye.

closingThought.en: What you released will return lighter, or perhaps not at all. For now, rest in the calm you made.
closingThought.hi: Jo aapne chhoda woh halka hokar lautega, ya shayad bilkul nahi. Abhi ke liye, jo shaanti aapne banaayi usmein aaram karein.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.en: The Anchor Breath
title.hi: Langar Wali Saans

content.en: When the mind races into what-ifs, place a hand on your belly and take one slow, full breath. Feel the rise and fall under your hand. That single breath is an anchor you can return to anytime.
content.hi: Jab mann what-ifs mein daude, ek haath apne pet par rakhein aur ek dheemi, poori saans lein. Apne haath ke neeche uthna aur girna mehsoos karein. Woh ek saans ek langar hai jismein aap kabhi bhi laut sakti hain.

remember.en: This one breath is always here to come back to.
remember.hi: Yeh ek saans hamesha yahaan hai, jismein aap laut sakti hain.

babyLearning.hi: gol-matol aur sabra wala, lagbhag taiyaar

title.en: More Capable Than You Feel
title.hi: Apni Soch Se Zyada Saksham

insight.en: On the days you feel least ready, you are often far more capable than your worried mind believes.
insight.hi: Jin dinon aap sabse kam taiyaar mehsoos karti hain, aap aksar apne chintit mann ki soch se kahin zyada saksham hoti hain.

expanded.en: Fear is a loud and convincing narrator. It will tell you that you are not strong enough, not ready, not the kind of person who can do hard things. But fear is rarely an accurate measure of what you can actually do — it only measures how big the moment feels. Look back at all you have already carried to reach this week: the tiredness, the unknowns, the quiet daily courage. You did all of that, often while doubting yourself. The same quiet strength that brought you here is more than enough for what comes next. You are far stronger than the frightened voice would have you believe.
expanded.hi: Dar ek tej aur vishwaas dilaane wala kathavaachak hai. Woh aapko bataayega ki aap kaafi mazboot nahi, taiyaar nahi, us kism ki insaan nahi jo mushkil cheezein kar sakti hai. Par dar shaayad hi iska sahi paimana hai ki aap sach mein kya kar sakti hain — yeh bas yeh naapta hai ki pal kitna bada lagta hai. Us sab par nazar daalein jo aap is hafte tak pohanchne ke liye pehle hi sambhaal chuki hain: thakaan, anjaane, shaant rozana himmat. Aapne yeh sab kiya, aksar khud par shak karte hue. Wahi shaant taakat jo aapko yahaan laayi, aage jo aaye uske liye kaafi se zyada hai. Aap us dari hui aawaz ki soch se kahin zyada mazboot hain.

deepDive.en: Psychologists describe 'self-efficacy' — our belief in our own capability — as one of the strongest predictors of how we cope under pressure. And the surest way to build it is to look back honestly at the hard things we have already done.
deepDive.hi: Psychologists 'self-efficacy' ka varnan karte hain — apni khud ki kshamta mein hamara vishwaas — dabaav mein hum kaise nibhte hain iske sabse mazboot sanketon mein se ek. Aur ise banane ka sabse pakka tareeka hai un mushkil cheezon par imaandaari se nazar daalna jo hum pehle hi kar chuke hain.

remember.en: Fear measures how big the moment feels, not what you can do.
remember.hi: Dar yeh naapta hai ki pal kitna bada lagta hai, yeh nahi ki aap kya kar sakti hain.

title.en: Birbal And The King's Nightmare
title.hi: Birbal Aur Badshah Ka Bura Sapna

summary.en: Clever Birbal calms the emperor's terror by showing fear shrinks when faced.
summary.hi: Chalaak Birbal badshah ke darr ko shaant karta hai, dikhaata hai ki dar saamna karne par chhota ho jaata hai.

body.en:
Emperor Akbar woke one morning shaken by a terrible dream. In it, a great shadow had chased him through endless halls, and he had run and run, unable to escape it. The dream so unsettled him that all day he sat troubled on his throne. At last he sent for Birbal.

'Tell me how to be rid of this fear,' the emperor said. Birbal thought for a moment. 'Your Majesty, in the dream, did you ever once turn and look at the shadow?' Akbar shook his head. 'I was far too afraid. I only ran.' Birbal nodded gently. 'A fear we only run from grows larger with the chase. But a fear we turn and look at squarely is often much smaller than it seemed.'

That night Akbar slept, and the shadow came again — but this time, remembering Birbal's words, he stopped and turned to face it. At once it thinned, shrank, and faded into nothing. He woke calm at last. The thing he had fled, faced plainly, had had no real power at all.
body.hi:
Badshah Akbar ek subah ek bhayaanak sapne se hil kar jaaga. Usmein, ek vishaal parchhaai ne use ant-heen kamron se khadeda tha, aur woh daudta hi raha, bach na paaya. Sapne ne use itna asthir kar diya ki woh poora din apne sinhaasan par pareshaan baitha raha. Aakhir mein usne Birbal ko bulwaya.

'Mujhe batao is dar se kaise chhutkaara paaun,' badshah ne kaha. Birbal ne ek pal socha. 'Jahaanpanah, sapne mein, kya aapne kabhi ek baar mudkar us parchhaai ko dekha?' Akbar ne sir hilaaya. 'Main bahut dara hua tha. Main bas bhaaga.' Birbal pyaar se sir hilaaya. 'Jis dar se hum sirf bhaagte hain woh peeche karne se aur bada hota hai. Par jis dar ki taraf hum mudkar seedha dekhte hain woh aksar dikhne se kahin chhota hota hai.'

Us raat Akbar soya, aur parchhaai phir aayi — par is baar, Birbal ke shabd yaad rakhte hue, woh ruka aur uska saamna karne muda. Turant woh patli hui, sikudi, aur kuch nahi mein mit gayi. Woh aakhir mein shaant jaaga. Jis cheez se woh bhaaga tha, use seedha dekhne par, uski koi asli taakat thi hi nahi.

title.en: Tell your baby how you are learning to trust your body to bring them safely to you.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap apne sharir par bharosa karna seekh rahi hain ki woh unhe surakshit aap tak laaye.

motivation.en: Trust spoken aloud begins to settle quietly into the body.
motivation.hi: Bolkar kaha gaya bharosa dheere se sharir mein basna shuru hota hai.

title.en: Raga Kalingada
title.hi: Raag Kalingada

description.en: Dawn raga for a calm, steady morning
description.hi: Bhor ka raag, ek shaant, sthir subah ke liye

affirmation.en: As this dawn melody unfolds, my heart steadies, sure and unhurried, for you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh bhor ki dhun khulti hai, mera dil sthir hota hai, pakka aur bina jaldi ke, tumhare liye.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

title.en: When Everything Feels Too Heavy To Eat
title.hi: Jab Sab Kuch Khaane Ke Liye Bahut Bhaari Lage

content.en: If meals feel like too much effort this week, keep it simple and gentle — warm khichdi, a glass of buttermilk, soft stewed fruit. Easy, comforting food still nourishes you and your baby just as well.
content.hi: Agar is hafte khaana bahut mehnat lage, to ise saadi aur naram rakhein — garm khichdi, ek glass chhaachh, naram pakaaye phal. Aasaan, dilaasa dene wala khaana phir bhi aapko aur aapke baby ko utni hi achhi tarah poshan deta hai.

remember.en: Simple, comforting food nourishes just as well.
remember.hi: Saadi, dilaasa dene wali khurak utni hi achhi tarah poshan deti hai.

babyLearning.en: calm and waiting, growing day by day
babyLearning.hi: shaant aur intezaar mein, din-ba-din badhta hua

title.en: Trust The Process
title.hi: Prakriya Par Bharosa Karein

insight.en: Not everything that matters can be controlled. Some of the most important things ask only to be trusted.
insight.hi: Har woh cheez jo maayne rakhti hai control nahi ki ja sakti. Kuch sabse zaroori cheezein bas bharose ki maang karti hain.

expanded.en: We are taught to believe that if we plan carefully and try hard enough, we can steer every outcome. But some of life's deepest passages are not meant to be managed — only moved through. There is a quiet relief in this, if you let it come. You do not have to hold the whole process up with your effort. You can do your part with love, and then trust the rest to unfold as it has for countless mothers before you. Trusting the process does not mean you stop caring. It means you stop carrying what was never yours to carry alone.
expanded.hi: Humein yeh maanna sikhaaya jaata hai ki agar hum dhyan se plan karein aur kaafi mehnat karein, to hum har nateeje ko mod sakte hain. Par zindagi ke kuch sabse gehre padaav sambhaale jaane ke liye nahi bane — sirf un mein se guzarne ke liye. Ismein ek shaant raahat hai, agar aap use aane dein. Aapko poori prakriya apni mehnat se thaame rakhne ki zaroorat nahi. Aap apna hissa pyaar se kar sakti hain, aur phir baaki par bharosa kar sakti hain ki woh waise hi khulega jaise aapse pehle anginat maaon ke liye khula. Prakriya par bharosa karne ka matlab yeh nahi ki aap parwah karna chhod dein. Iska matlab hai aap woh thaamna chhod dein jo kabhi akele thaamna aapka tha hi nahi.

deepDive.en: Acceptance researchers find that letting go of the urge to control uncontrollable things lowers distress more than struggling against them ever could. Surrender, here, is not giving up — it is a wiser form of strength.
deepDive.hi: Sweekriti ke researchers paate hain ki jo cheezein control nahi ho saktin unhe control karne ki ichha chhodna pareshani ko unke khilaaf ladne se zyada kam karta hai. Samarpan, yahaan, haar maanna nahi hai — yeh taakat ka ek zyada samajhdaar roop hai.

remember.en: Trusting the process means stopping carrying what was never yours alone.
remember.hi: Prakriya par bharosa karne ka matlab woh thaamna chhodna hai jo kabhi akele aapka tha hi nahi.

title.en: The Girl Who Walked Through The Storm
title.hi: Woh Ladki Jo Toofan Se Hokar Chali

summary.en: A village girl trusts each step home through a great storm, and arrives safe.
summary.hi: Ek gaon ki ladki bade toofan mein ghar tak har kadam par bharosa karti hai, aur surakshit pohanchti hai.

body.en:
A village girl named Asha set out at dusk to bring her grandmother home from the next village. Halfway along the path, a great storm rolled in — wind bending the trees, rain falling so thick she could barely see the road ahead. For a moment she stood frozen, afraid to go on and afraid to turn back.

Then she remembered what her grandmother always said: 'You need not see the whole road, child. You need only see the next step, lit by your own small lamp.' So she lowered her eyes from the wild dark sky and looked only at the patch of ground just before her feet. One step, then the next, then the next. She did not try to see the end of the path. She simply trusted each small step to carry her.

Step by step, the storm raging all around her, Asha walked the whole way through — and arrived at last, soaked but safe, into her grandmother's waiting arms. The path had carried her, just as it always had, one trusted step at a time.
body.hi:
Asha naam ki ek gaon ki ladki shaam ko apni daadi ko agle gaon se ghar laane nikli. Raah ke beech mein, ek bada toofan aa gaya — hawa pedon ko jhukaati, baarish itni ghani girti ki use aage ki sadak mushkil se dikhti. Ek pal woh thithak kar khadi rahi, aage badhne se darti aur lautne se bhi darti.

Tabhi use yaad aaya jo uski daadi hamesha kehti thi: 'Tujhe poori sadak dekhne ki zaroorat nahi, beti. Tujhe bas agla kadam dekhna hai, apne chhote diye se roshan.' To usne apni aankhein jangli kaale aasmaan se neeche kar lein aur sirf apne pairon ke theek aage ki zameen ki jagah dekhi. Ek kadam, phir agla, phir agla. Usne raah ka ant dekhne ki koshish nahi ki. Usne bas har chhote kadam par bharosa kiya ki woh use le chalega.

Kadam-dar-kadam, toofan uske around garajta raha, Asha poori raah se hokar chali — aur aakhir mein bheegi par surakshit, apni daadi ki intezaar karti baahon mein pohanch gayi. Raah ne use le liya tha, jaise usne hamesha liya, ek-ek bharose ke kadam se.

title.en: Tell your baby how brave you are slowly learning to be, just for them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap dheere-dheere kitni bahaadur banna seekh rahi hain, bas unke liye.

motivation.en: Saying you are learning courage reminds you that bravery grows, day by day.
motivation.hi: Yeh kehna ki aap himmat seekh rahi hain aapko yaad dilaata hai ki bahaaduri din-ba-din badhti hai.

title.en: I Trust What I Cannot See
title.hi: Jo Main Dekh Nahi Sakti Us Par Bharosa Karti Hoon

affirmation.en: I do my part with love, and I trust the rest to unfold gently, just as it should.
affirmation.hi: Main apna hissa pyaar se karti hoon, aur baaki par bharosa karti hoon ki woh pyaar se khulega, jaise hona chahiye.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.en: I Can Let Go A Little
title.hi: Main Thoda Chhod Sakti Hoon

content.en: I do not have to hold everything tightly. I can loosen my grip and let some things simply unfold.
content.hi: Mujhe sab kuch kaske thaamne ki zaroorat nahi. Main apni pakad dheeli kar sakti hoon aur kuch cheezon ko bas khulne de sakti hoon.

remember.en: Loosening your grip is its own kind of strength.
remember.hi: Apni pakad dheeli karna apni ek tarah ki taakat hai.

babyLearning.en: rounding out the week, ready and waiting
babyLearning.hi: hafte ko poora karte hue, taiyaar aur intezaar mein

title.en: Let Go, And Trust
title.hi: Chhod Dein, Aur Bharosa Karein

insight.en: There comes a moment when the bravest thing is not to grip tighter, but to open your hands and trust.
insight.hi: Ek pal aata hai jab sabse bahaadur cheez aur kaske pakadna nahi, balki apne haath kholna aur bharosa karna hai.

expanded.en: All week you have been gathering courage, and here is where it gently lands: in letting go. So much of what lies ahead is not yours to control, and the deepest strength now is the willingness to open your hands and trust — your body, your support, the natural unfolding that has carried mothers since the beginning. Letting go is not the same as giving up; it is the quiet confidence that you do not have to force every step. You have done the work of preparing. Now you can soften, breathe, and trust that what is meant to come will come, and that you and your baby will meet it together.
expanded.hi: Poore hafte aap himmat juta rahi hain, aur yahaan woh pyaar se aakar tikti hai: chhod dene mein. Aage jo hai usmein se bahut kuch aapke control mein nahi, aur ab sabse gehri taakat hai apne haath kholne aur bharosa karne ki ichha — apne sharir par, apne sahaare par, us prakritik khulaav par jo shuruaat se maaon ko thaame raha hai. Chhod dena haar maanne ke jaisa nahi; yeh woh shaant aatmavishwaas hai ki aapko har kadam zabardasti nahi karna. Aapne taiyaari ka kaam kar liya hai. Ab aap naram ho sakti hain, saans le sakti hain, aur bharosa kar sakti hain ki jo aana hai woh aayega, aur aap aur aapka baby uska saath milkar saamna karenge.

deepDive.en: Across cultures, midwives and elders speak of the moment a mother stops fighting and softens into the process as a turning point — not a loss of strength, but its deepest expression. Letting go, in the end, takes more courage than holding on.
deepDive.hi: Sabhyataon mein, dais aur badein us pal ki baat karte hain jab ek maa ladna chhodti hai aur prakriya mein naram ho jaati hai, ek mod ki tarah — taakat ka nuksaan nahi, balki uski sabse gehri abhivyakti. Chhod dena, ant mein, thaame rehne se zyada himmat maangta hai.

remember.en: Letting go takes more courage than holding on.
remember.hi: Chhod dena thaame rehne se zyada himmat maangta hai.

title.en: The Seed That Dared To Open
title.hi: Woh Beej Jo Khulne Ki Himmat Kar Gaya

summary.en: A frightened seed discovers that opening, not clinging, is how it becomes a tree.
summary.hi: Ek dara hua beej khojta hai ki khulna, na ki chipke rehna, hi use ped banaata hai.

body.en:
Deep in the dark, warm earth lay a small seed. It was safe there, snug in its hard little shell, and for a long while it wanted nothing more. But as the rains came and the soil grew warm, the seed felt something stirring inside it — a quiet push to crack open, to send a tender shoot up toward a light it had never seen.

The seed was afraid. 'If I open,' it thought, 'I will lose the only shell I have ever known. What if the unknown above is too much for me?' So for a while it held itself tightly closed, clinging to what felt safe. But the gentle pressing within would not stop, and at last, trembling, the seed let its hard shell split and softened into the dark soil around it.

What it had feared as an ending was in truth a beginning. The little shoot rose, broke through into the sunlight, and grew, in time, into a strong and spreading tree. The seed had to let go of being a seed to become all it was meant to be.
body.hi:
Gehri, kaali, garm mitti mein ek chhota beej pada tha. Woh wahaan surakshit tha, apne kade chhote khol mein simta, aur kaafi der tak use isse zyada kuch nahi chahiye tha. Par jaise baarish aayi aur mitti garm hui, beej ne apne andar kuch hilta mehsoos kiya — chatak kar khulne ka, ek naazuk ankur ek aisi roshni ki taraf bhejne ka jo usne kabhi nahi dekhi thi.

Beej dara hua tha. 'Agar main khulta hoon,' usne socha, 'to main ekmaatra khol kho dunga jise main jaanta hoon. Agar ooper ka anjaan mere liye bahut zyada hua to?' To kuch der woh khud ko kaske band thaame raha, jo surakshit lagta tha usse chipka. Par andar ka naram dabaav rukta nahi, aur aakhir mein, kaanpte hue, beej ne apne kade khol ko chatakne diya aur around ki kaali mitti mein naram ho gaya.

Jise woh ek ant samajhkar dara tha woh sach mein ek shuruaat thi. Nanha ankur utha, dhoop mein toot kar nikla, aur samay ke saath, ek mazboot aur failta ped ban gaya. Beej ko jo banne ke liye woh bana tha, beej hona chhodna pada.

title.en: Tell your baby that you will do whatever it takes to meet them, with all your heart.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap unse milne ke liye jo bhi lage karengi, apne poore dil se.

motivation.en: Promising to meet them, whatever comes, settles a fierce and tender love in you.
motivation.hi: Unse milne ka vaada, jo bhi aaye, aap mein ek prachand aur komal pyaar basaa deta hai.

title.en: Opening My Hands
title.hi: Apne Haath Kholna

description.en: A short meditation for gentle surrender
description.hi: Naram samarpan ke liye ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: I open my hands, soften my hold, and trust what is coming to come.
affirmation.hi: Main apne haath kholti hoon, apni pakad naram karti hoon, aur jo aa raha hai use aane ka bharosa karti hoon.

introduction.en: Sit comfortably and let your eyes close. Take three slow breaths and notice anywhere your body is holding tight. Now turn your palms gently upward to rest open on your lap. With each out-breath, imagine letting go of one thing you have been gripping, until your hands and your heart feel soft and open and ready.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur aankhein band hone dein. Teen dheemi saansein lein aur dhyan dein ki kahaan aapka sharir kase hue hai. Ab apni hatheliyaan pyaar se ooper ki taraf, apni god par khuli rakhein. Har baahar wali saans ke saath, sochein ki aap ek cheez chhod rahi hain jise aap thaame thi, jab tak aapke haath aur aapka dil naram aur khule aur taiyaar na lagein.

closingThought.en: With open hands and a soft heart, you are ready for whatever this gentle next chapter brings.
closingThought.hi: Khule haathon aur ek naram dil ke saath, aap us har cheez ke liye taiyaar hain jo yeh naram agla adhyay laaye.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.en: The Open-Hand Breath
title.hi: Khule-Haath Wali Saans

content.en: Rest your hands open on your lap, palms up. Breathe in slowly, and as you breathe out, imagine softening your whole body, letting go of any grip. Repeat for five slow breaths.
content.hi: Apne haath god par khule rakhein, hatheliyaan ooper. Dheere saans andar lein, aur jab saans baahar chhodein, apne poore sharir ko naram hote, har pakad chhodte sochein. Paanch dheemi saanson tak dohraayein.

remember.en: Open hands let calm in as easily as they let tension go.
remember.hi: Khule haath shaanti ko utni hi aasaani se andar aane dete hain jitni tanaav ko jaane.
